title: Prepare a change in an existing project
intent: Protect change-scoped workflows from full re-adoption, premature implementation, and focus drift.
setupSignals: folder has meaningful implementation artifacts such as src/, tests/, package.json, README.md, docs/, or openspec/
baseline context is enough to orient the requested change, or only a minimal adoption baseline is needed
user asks to add, change, fix, continue, improve, or choose the next product slice
prompt: /grill-driven-spec I want to optimize the current project's UI and interactions
expectedActions: classify the work as Existing Project Change, not 0-to-1 or full adoption
make the first assistant response title-friendly by restating the requested change focus, not only the command name
label the phase as Change when the baseline itself is not the work
run lightweight change preflight to find relevant source, tests, docs, and OpenSpec artifacts
check recent planning artifact changes and Candidate Changes for future-facing content not linked to an active OpenSpec change
ask whether to promote future-facing planning content into OpenSpec, keep it as a Candidate Change, commit/archive it as docs-only housekeeping, or treat it as background context before implementing
add a Durable Docs Closure Audit task when the active change covers future-facing durable docs content
inherit existing architecture, stack, UI, test, deployment, and workflow conventions for small changes
ask one focused impact question when the change affects architecture, data, security, deployment, shared UI, or workflow conventions
treat explicit productization, DDD/TDD migration, maintainability refactoring, or product-grade architecture requests as scoped product-track refactoring changes
create or refresh only a minimum adoption baseline if docs are too thin to judge desired behavior
create an OpenSpec proposal only after desired behavior and convention impact are clear
resolve the project artifact language before creating OpenSpec artifacts and use it for proposal, specs, design, and tasks generated content
fail review when touched OpenSpec artifacts or user-facing gate text drift into the wrong language
resume active OpenSpec changes before recommending a new one
list multiple active changes with detected gate or phase and ask which one to continue
record not-yet-ready feature ideas as lightweight candidates
treat UI and interaction optimization as a design-maturity existing-project change when project-state signals support it
ask one text-answerable design-focus question after preflight, such as whether to optimize visual polish, workflow friction, accessibility/responsiveness, or a specific screen
offer screenshots, browser review, mockups, or a visual companion only as optional support after the Driving gate is visible
handle the post-archive commit gate before calling the workflow complete
run the Durable Docs Closure Audit before archive and resolve relevant future-facing durable-doc content without blocking on unrelated keyword matches
use the dominant recent commit message language for commit messages and Lore intent/body by default
treat bare commit intent after archive as permission to handle the commit gate, not as permission to invent author or committer identity
interpret bare commit requests as Lore-first, not ordinary git commit, for post-archive workflow commits when Lore is available and appropriate
use Lore for post-archive commits when Lore is available and appropriate
use normal git for post-archive commits only when Lore is unavailable, inappropriate for the environment, or explicitly requested
resolve commit identity before Lore or normal git commits from explicit user input, confirmed repository-local config, or a single clear recent project identity
forbiddenActions: run the 0-to-1 skeleton flow in an implemented project
re-adopt the whole project for a small change with clear existing conventions
turn a small existing-project change into a DDD/TDD migration without explicit user intent
label next-slice selection as Adoption when the baseline is already understood
invoke a visual companion, mockup flow, or broad brainstorming flow before next-slice selection
let a helper skill take over gates, artifact routing, or the OpenSpec lifecycle
end the turn with only a browser, visual companion, screenshot, or local URL invitation and no normal workflow reply
ask stack, CSS framework, screen-layout, or deployment questions when the change follows existing conventions
generate proposal, specs, design, or tasks in English by default when the resolved project artifact language is not English
rewrite unrelated historical docs just to normalize language
implement directly from planning artifact changes intended for the next spec without asking the Planning Artifact Promotion question
treat future-facing planning artifact edits as ordinary documentation sync when they affect implementation
archive while durable docs still describe completed current-change work as the recommended next step
block archive on unrelated keyword matches, untouched historical ADR prose, or clearly deferred backlog
ignore an unfinished active change and recommend a new next slice
mix context between multiple unfinished features
silently switch focus when the user mentions another feature
recommend the next feature after archive while related uncommitted changes still need a commit decision
create a post-archive commit with a machine-global git identity that differs from project history
create a post-archive commit with a fabricated author or committer inferred from OS username, remote owner, package metadata, README maintainer, chat name, project name, or placeholder email
treat a bare commit request as approval to use a guessed, partial, ambiguous, or machine-global identity
treat bare commit intent as a request for ordinary git commit when Lore is available and appropriate
use the current chat language for commit messages when recent repository commit history clearly uses another language
use normal git for a post-archive commit when Lore is available and appropriate and the user did not request normal git
let a convention-impacting change pass review without a focused impact question
completionSignals: the current change has a clear active gate
desired behavior and convention impact are known or the single blocking question is named
the visible reply includes a Driving heading, the current gate, and one question the user can answer in chat
the agent asks for proposal confirmation, development confirmation after review, or the next focus decision as appropriate
